title: Why is Classified Information accepted as gospel
display_title: Why is Classified Information accepted as gospel
wikitext:
[]: Why is Classified Information accepted as gospel?             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Sat Nov 06, 2010 3:36 pm We can all agree that Eva 2 is noncanon, so why do we randomly accept a boring, contradictory list of statements as canon? If it's because it had Anno's involvement, than why are we ignoring the rest of the game? It's like when people write off Leviticus as being outdated, but still use it as evidence against homosexuality. It doesn't make sense.

[]:             Quote Post by Jinroh » Sat Nov 06, 2010 4:04 pm CI is just material eva 2 is based on. I don't really understand your point. Anno didn't make the game.

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Sat Nov 06, 2010 4:06 pm CI is based on interviews with Anno.

[]:             Quote Post by chaosakita » Sat Nov 06, 2010 4:46 pm lol Please tell me where its contradictory. And CI : Eva :: Leviticus : Homosexuality? Tell me about STRAW MAN. And obviously an anime is comparable to an important issue that affects uncountable amount of people in real life.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Sat Nov 06, 2010 4:48 pm Allemann wrote: CI is based on interviews with Anno.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sat Nov 06, 2010 6:59 pm TehDonutKing wrote: So is the rest of the game....

[]:             Quote Post by Fazmotron » Sat Nov 06, 2010 6:59 pm This thread needs lots of SOURCE. As far as I know, the CI is created from interviews with Anno, but the game isn't. Edit: Beaten by Ornette.

[]: Re: Why is Classified Information accepted as gospel?             Quote Post by Anti-Goth » Sat Nov 06, 2010 7:29 pm OH BOY, HERE WE GO. Honestly? The CI isn't really relevant to the main themes of the show in the long run. So do you need it to properly enjoy Eva? Of course not. I for one don't really see what the big deal is.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Nov 06, 2010 7:45 pm Also, it's not as if you can't work out most of what's in the CI from clues in the show plus SF genre tropes. It mainly acts to confirm the deductions.

[]:             Quote Post by soul.assassin » Sat Nov 06, 2010 8:16 pm Furthermore, IMHO, some CI entries contradict other information in documents such as the Red Cross Book (which also puts it in the same category as the CI) . But honestly, what matters more is everyone's enjoyment of the show and the appreciation of its characters, unencumbered by what seems to be noise, excess and unnecessary information. However, as Eva is a science fiction story to me, it's expected of the genre to have a form of world-building.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Nov 06, 2010 9:26 pm Why? Because while the Eva2 game itself obviously isn't canon (although the characters do act mostly in-character with the exception of a few intentionally hilarious situations in the PSP port) , the Classified Information is. For what it's worth though, Anno/Gainax did work on the Events engine for the game (and probably out-lined the Scenarios, given how much sense they make for each of the characters). Translation of Eva2 canoncity wrote: These [mysteries] are addressed in the form of Confidential Information in the game. Basically, GAINAX as well as Hideaki Anno supervised. They didn't mind to speak up and it was a semi-formal setting. However, Alpha System's stance is "This information is absolutely reliable." compared to GAINAX'S comment "Enough information has been provided in the TV series and film. These are just the settings established for the game." and show the difference as a slight nuance. If one part of the game's section is altered, the original version's portrayal will clash with that section.

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Sat Nov 06, 2010 9:32 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: Why? Because while the Eva2 game itself obviously isn't canon (although the characters do act mostly in-character with the exception of a few intentionally hilarious situations in the PSP port) , the Classified Information is.

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Sat Nov 06, 2010 9:43 pm ...

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Sat Nov 06, 2010 9:48 pm chaosakita wrote: lol Please tell me where its contradictory. And CI : Eva :: Leviticus : Homosexuality? Tell me about STRAW MAN. And obviously an anime is comparable to an important issue that affects uncountable amount of people in real life.

[]:             Quote Post by IrkenEvangelion » Sat Nov 06, 2010 9:59 pm EvaWiki wrote: It is generally considered a cornerstone of supplemental canon for the influx of new information it provides and given a great deal of weight due to Hideaki Anno's participation in the game's production. While most of the information reconciles fully with the show, there are some instances of apparent conflict. It is unclear to what extent this represents genuine inconsistency as opposed to reflecting disparities in security access (e.g., it requires a higher clearance level to learn that the white giant is Lilith rather than Adam). The CI could be considered to fall somewhere between tier 2 or tier 3 canon, due to the fact that there is no way of confirming that the information is "100% Anno" and wasn't embellished at all by the game's producers.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sun Nov 07, 2010 6:40 pm soul.assassin wrote: Furthermore, IMHO, some CI entries contradict other information in documents such as the Red Cross Book.

[]:             Quote Post by soul.assassin » Sun Nov 07, 2010 6:51 pm AuraTwilight wrote: You say that as if anyone actually thought the Red Cross Book was even slightly reliable. HERP A DERP NAOKO'S IN UNIT 00 DURHP

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sun Nov 07, 2010 7:16 pm Where in the RCB does it say that Naoko is in Unit-00?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Nov 07, 2010 7:50 pm What I was going to add to the issue of CI/canon is that the CI just adds extended information of what we already know in the series. It's just more background, additional information. (For instance: the FAR are hinted at in Episode 21. And a draft of either episode 20 or 21 had Misato mull over the FAR and the Angels so it's not like the FAR were thought up 10 years after Eva ended!) I'm not sure why CI canoncity should be such an issue. Regarding whether or not the Eva2 game is canon... While the video game obviously isn't within the same situations of the series (unless you're playing Shinji Scenario 1 which is of NGE/EoE events and even with that, the Good End is different from the EoE Bad End), the character Scenarios of the cast are rather fitting for them all. Of course, more game events have to be translated. And like I said before, a few character interaction events such as Kaji (who wears Misato's clothes to teach Shinji how to get girls >_> ) and Fuyutsuki (who dresses Shinji up in various outfits because of his Yui-obsession) in Shinji's 2nd PSP Scenario are meant to be played for laughs. Nothing wrong with humor, especially in a usually depressing series like Eva. Anyway, I was requested to pass on this POV from Reichu (since she has a self-imposed "Not posting" rule): Reichu wrote: Note that what's under consideration are not only the CI files within the game, but the game in general. The CI were the most convenient and intuitive item to translate first, due to their ability to stand alone. However, in focusing on the CI all these years, it's been easy to overlook the fact that there's additional information scattered throughout the rest of the game. As demonstrated by the scene I recently translated (involving Kaworu's conversation with another Seed), even information presented in the game itself can supplement what we know in a very straightforward way. Examples of info-dumping that would resolve various questions appear to be all over. As a random example, in a Rei scenario I hope to tackle soon, Lilith tells Rei to give her the Fruit of Life (or some such), which implies (A) Lilith does not have the Fruit of Life (this debate still has to happen from time to time...) , and (B) some form of consciousness has remained inside Lilith all along. [But wait for the full translation before debating. Context might be important...] This is only the tip of the iceberg. Of course, we wouldn't simply have common disputes settled; the new material (such as alternate depictions of Third Impact) would give us new things to talk about, and alternate ways of looking at the same old. [Snip] We need to be able to coexist peacefully. Obviously on an Eva forum all those extra goodies cannot be ignored, so for the sake of community and effective communication the artsy fundamentalists lose out. The tier ranking system is here to stay. This is where it might be useful to partition things in your head: seek an answer that's "right" in the community setting, and seek one that's "right" for you -- analogous to how some scientists can stick to the rules in their professional work, but practice Potatoism in their off time. Alternatively, you could just ignore everyone and everything contributing to the decadence of philosophical attitudes towards artistic works.

[]:             Quote Post by Dirty Mick » Sun Nov 07, 2010 8:59 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: And like I said before, a few character interaction events such as Kaji (who wears Misato's clothes to teach Shinji how to get girls >_> )

If it's because it had Anno's involvement, than why are we ignoring the rest of the game? It's like when people write off Leviticus as being outdated, but still use it as evidence against h
